subject: Physics
title: Series vs Parallel Circuits
description: A quick win: understand Series vs Parallel Circuits.

hook.text: Old Christmas lights went dark when one bulb blew. Modern ones stay lit. The difference is circuit design.

buildup.text: In a series circuit, current flows through each component one after another—a single path.

discovery.text: If one bulb breaks in series, the circuit breaks and all bulbs go dark. One failure kills everything.

twist.text: In a parallel circuit, each component has its own path to the battery. One failure doesn't affect the others.

climax.text: Your home wiring is parallel—so turning off one light doesn't shut down the whole house.

punchline.text: One path or many paths. That choice decides if one failure takes down all.

quiz.question: Why does your home use parallel circuits?
options: So one device failing doesn't affect the others
Because parallel circuits use less energy
Because series circuits are more expensive
